metadata.topic: Public AI control baseline for internal generative AI pilots
metadata.scope: Compact decision memo using only public official or framework sources

inferred_research_questions.question: Which public source supports using Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage as AI risk-management framing?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The memo should not invent its own governance framing.
search_queries: NIST AI Risk Management Framework Govern Map Measure Manage

inferred_research_questions.question: Which public source supports treating generative AI risks as requiring profile-specific handling?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The baseline should explain why generic controls are not enough.

inferred_research_questions.question: Which public source supports prompt injection as a specific LLM application risk?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The pilot baseline needs a concrete LLM security control area.

inferred_research_questions.question: Which public source supports lifecycle security controls for AI systems?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: The baseline should cover design, deployment, and operation, not only model behavior.
search_queries: NCSC CISA Guidelines for secure AI system development

inferred_research_questions.question: What operating mechanism makes the baseline governable after approval?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: A baseline without repeatable evidence fields, status values, and recertification gates becomes a one-time memo rather than governance.

inferred_research_questions.question: What accountability model makes pilot attestations reviewable after approval?
inferred_research_questions.why_it_matters: A baseline needs named operating and validation responsibilities, not only evidence fields.
search_queries: AI risk governance accountability secure AI lifecycle responsibilities

research_plan.depth_rationale: The paper is an internal decision memo with control consequences, so each material claim needs a public source ID.
research_plan.source_mode_rationale: This example intentionally uses live public sources verified on 2026-05-12.
research_plan.user_feedback: Keep the example compact and source-fidelity focused.

research_brief.executive_summary: The public sources support a defensible minimum baseline for internal generative AI pilots: AI risk framing from NIST AI RMF, generative AI risk profile considerations from NIST AI 600-1, prompt-injection control focus from OWASP, and lifecycle security practices from NCSC/CISA guidance.
key_findings: NIST AI RMF provides Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage as risk-management functions.
NIST AI 600-1 is a generative AI profile for the AI RMF and supports profile-specific handling.
OWASP LLM01 identifies prompt injection as a specific LLM application risk.
NCSC/CISA guidance organizes secure AI system development across design, development, deployment, and operation.

source_registry.title: NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)

claim_support.rationale: The framework defines the core Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions used by the memo.
The framework supports governance and evidence framing, but not the memo's exact pilot control record schema.
The framework supports governance accountability, but not the memo's exact internal role names.

source_registry.why_picked: Selected because it is a official source with high relevance and high specificity for this paper.
source_registry.use_in_paper: Use for mapped claim support and source-traceable evidence in this paper.

source_registry.title: NIST AI 600-1 Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: NIST profile for generative AI risk management.

claim_support.rationale: The publication is a generative AI profile of the AI RMF and supports profile-specific treatment.

source_registry.notes: Verified NIST publication page and DOI on 2026-05-12.
source_registry.why_picked: Selected because it is a official source with high relevance and high specificity for this paper.

source_registry.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Community security framework focused on generative AI application risk.

claim_support.rationale: The source defines prompt injection as an LLM application risk and describes unintended model behavior.

source_registry.notes: Verified public OWASP GenAI page on 2026-05-12.
source_registry.why_picked: Selected because it is a official source with high relevance and high specificity for this paper.
source_registry.short_summary: Verified public OWASP GenAI page on 2026-05-12.

source_registry.title: NCSC Guidelines for Secure AI System Development
source_registry.bias_or_agenda: Government cybersecurity guidance co-sealed with CISA and international partners.

claim_support.rationale: The guidance is organized around secure design, secure development, secure deployment, and secure operation and maintenance.
The guidance supports lifecycle controls, but the record ID, enum, and evidence template are internal operating-design proposals.
The guidance supports lifecycle security responsibilities, but the exact accountability table is an internal operating-design proposal.

source_registry.notes: Verified public NCSC guidance page on 2026-05-12.

evidence_matrix.claim: NIST AI RMF supports organizing AI risk management through Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions.
Generative AI needs profile-specific risk handling beyond generic AI governance.
evidence_matrix.notes: Keep with wording that the profile supports, not mandates, the baseline.

evidence_matrix.claim: Prompt injection should be treated as a specific pilot control topic.
evidence_matrix.notes: Keep as one baseline control, not the only security requirement.

evidence_matrix.claim: Secure AI guidance supports lifecycle controls across design, development, deployment, and operation.
A baseline is only governable if it creates a repeatable pilot control record with evidence fields, status values, validation gates, and recertification triggers.
evidence_matrix.notes: Keep as internal operating-design proposal supported by governance and lifecycle framing, not as a public-standard requirement.

evidence_matrix.claim: A pilot baseline needs named roles for operating the control record and validating attestations.
evidence_matrix.notes: Keep as internal accountability design supported by governance and lifecycle framing, not as a public-source role mandate.

source_summaries.S1: NIST AI RMF provides risk-management functions that can frame pilot review.

rows.implication: Use NIST AI RMF as the organizing frame.

source_summaries.S2: NIST AI 600-1 gives generative AI-specific risk profile guidance.

rows.implication: Treat generative AI pilots as needing profile-specific checks.

source_summaries.S3: OWASP LLM01 identifies prompt injection as a material LLM application risk.

rows.implication: Require prompt-injection threat modeling and tests.

source_summaries.S4: NCSC/CISA guidance covers secure design, development, deployment, and operation.

rows.implication: Make lifecycle checks part of pilot approval.

source_summaries.S1: NIST AI RMF supports explicit governance framing for AI risk management.
source_summaries.S4: NCSC/CISA guidance supports security responsibilities across the AI lifecycle.

rows.implication: Name the internal roles that operate the control record and validate attestations.

contradictions.summary: No contradicting public source was added because the claims are limited to source framing, profile-specific risk attention, prompt-injection risk, secure lifecycle guidance, and clearly labeled internal operating-design proposals. The paper does not claim internal outcome proof.

open_questions: Which actual internal teams would fill the generic operating and validation roles in a real deployment?
draft_support_notes: Use source IDs beside each material framework claim.
Do not claim that the public sources prove the internal baseline will reduce incidents.
Keep implementation details out of this decision memo.
Define the pilot control record as an internal governance object, not as something required by the public sources.
Name accountability roles generically and avoid implying that public sources assign internal owners.
facts_safe_to_use: NIST AI RMF provides Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions.
NIST AI 600-1 is a generative AI profile for the AI RMF.
OWASP LLM01 treats prompt injection as an LLM application risk.
NCSC/CISA guidance frames secure AI development across the lifecycle.
claims_to_soften: The baseline should reduce risk if implemented and maintained; the public sources do not prove internal outcome reduction.
claims_to_drop_or_reframe: The baseline guarantees secure generative AI pilots.
